raw_requirement: Generate application to greet user with proper time like Good Morning, Evening. And ask if he/she want to see Quote of the Day or Joke of the Day. And Reply.
normalised_requirement:
Build a web application that:
1. Detects or accepts the user's local time.
2. Greets the user with the appropriate time-of-day salutation (Good Morning / Good Afternoon / Good Evening / Good Night).
3. Prompts the user to choose between 'Quote of the Day' and 'Joke of the Day'.
4. Fetches and displays the selected content from an appropriate third-party API.
5. Handles API errors gracefully with a retry option.
gherkin_business:
Feature: Time-based greeting and daily content selection

  Background:
    Given the user has the application open
    And the application has detected the client device local time

  Scenario Outline: Greeting displayed based on time of day
    Given the local time is <time>
    When the application loads
    Then the greeting "<greeting>" is displayed to the user

    Examples:
      | time  | greeting          |
      | 06:00 | Good Morning      |
      | 13:00 | Good Afternoon    |
      | 19:00 | Good Evening      |
      | 23:00 | Good Night        |

  Scenario: User is prompted to select content after greeting
    Given the greeting has been displayed
    When the user views the interface
    Then the options "Quote of the Day" and "Joke of the Day" are presented

  Scenario: User receives a quote after selecting Quote of the Day
    Given the greeting and content options are displayed
    When the user selects "Quote of the Day"
    Then the application retrieves a quote from the Quote-of-the-Day API
    And the retrieved quote text is displayed in the same session

  Scenario: User receives a joke after selecting Joke of the Day
    Given the greeting and content options are displayed
    When the user selects "Joke of the Day"
    Then the application retrieves a joke from the Joke API
    And the retrieved joke text is displayed in the same session

  Scenario: API failure shows error and retry option
    Given the user has selected a content option
    When the corresponding API call fails
    Then a user-readable error message is displayed
    And the user is offered the option to retry the request

  Scenario: Retry after API failure retrieves and displays content
    Given an API failure message and retry option are shown
    When the user selects retry
    Then the application calls the API again
    And the retrieved content is displayed upon success

environment_options.rationale: The requirement for client-device local time detection, a single-session interactive UI with two user-selectable options, third-party API calls, and inline error-with-retry handling maps directly to a React component tree where time is read via the browser's Date API and API calls are made client-side or via lightweight server routes, all of which Next.js handles natively with minimal infrastructure.

The requirement's emphasis on a simple two-option prompt, single text response display, and retry flow within the same interface session is achievable with a thin Express server rendering HTML fragments via HTMX, keeping the stack lightweight given there is no stated need for complex client-side state.

FastAPI with Jinja2 templates can serve the time-based greeting (resolved client-side via a small JS snippet), render the two-option prompt, proxy calls to the Quote-of-the-Day or Joke API, and return user-readable error messages with a retry option, satisfying all stated functional requirements with a widely adopted Python stack.

selected_environment.rationale: The requirement for client-device local time detection, a single-session interactive UI with two user-selectable options, third-party API calls, and inline error-with-retry handling maps directly to a React component tree where time is read via the browser's Date API and API calls are made client-side or via lightweight server routes, all of which Next.js handles natively with minimal infrastructure.

spec_advice.rationale: Defines normative requirements for greeting logic, API calls, and error handling.
spec_advice.insight: The app has branching user flows and fallible external calls — RFC 2119 SHALL/SHOULD/MAY keywords make obligations unambiguous.
spec_advice.statement: Produce a Markdown RFC 2119 document with sections: # Purpose, # Definitions, # Requirements (with ## Greeting Logic, ## Content Selection, ## Error Handling sub-sections), # Constraints.

spec_advice.rationale: Specifies the REST API surface exposed by the backend to the frontend.
spec_advice.requirement_segments: API calls for quote/joke · error responses
spec_advice.insight: The frontend calls the backend for content; a typed contract prevents mismatch.
spec_advice.statement: Produce a valid OpenAPI 3.1 YAML with paths /api/quote and /api/joke, 200 and error responses, and shared ErrorResponse schema.

spec_advice.rationale: Validates runtime data shapes for API responses and state objects.
spec_advice.insight: Schemas enforce payload contracts at the boundary between third-party APIs and the application.
spec_advice.statement: Produce a Markdown document containing JSON Schema definitions for QuoteResponse, JokeResponse, and ErrorResponse.

spec_advice.rationale: Visualises system context and container boundaries.
spec_advice.insight: Two external systems (Quote API, Joke API) and one internal container (Next.js app) make the C4 context and container diagrams immediately useful.
spec_advice.statement: Produce a Structurizr DSL file with a workspace, system context diagram, and container diagram.

spec_artefacts.rfc2119:
# Constraints

## Time-Based Greeting

- The application MUST derive the greeting string from the client device's local time at the moment the greeting is rendered.
- The application MUST display the string "Good Morning" when the client local time is in the range 05:00–11:59 inclusive.
- The application MUST display the string "Good Afternoon" when the client local time is in the range 12:00–16:59 inclusive.
- The application MUST display the string "Good Evening" when the client local time is in the range 17:00–20:59 inclusive.
- The application MUST display the string "Good Night" when the client local time is in the range 21:00–04:59 inclusive.
- The four time ranges (05:00–11:59, 12:00–16:59, 17:00–20:59, 21:00–04:59) MUST collectively cover all 1,440 minutes of the 24-hour clock with no gaps and no overlaps.
- The application MUST NOT display more than one greeting string simultaneously. [implied]

## Content Option Presentation

- The application MUST present content options to the user within the same interface session in which the greeting is displayed. [implied]
- The application MUST remain within the same interface session after the user selects a content option; a full-page navigation that destroys session state MUST NOT be used to fulfil the selection. [implied]

## API Retrieval

- The application MUST initiate retrieval of content from the relevant third-party API upon user selection of a content option.
- The application MUST display the retrieved content within the same interface session in which the option was selected.
- The application MUST treat a failed response from either third-party API call as an error condition requiring the error handling behaviour specified in Error and Retry Handling. [implied]

## Error and Retry Handling

- The application MUST display a user-readable error message when any third-party API call fails.
- The error message MUST be expressed in natural language intelligible to a non-technical end user; raw error codes or stack traces MUST NOT be presented as the sole error output. [implied]
- The application MUST present a retry control to the user upon display of the error message.
- Activating the retry control MUST cause the application to re-issue the failed API request without requiring the user to navigate away from the current interface session.
- The retry control MUST remain accessible to the user for as long as the error message is displayed. [implied]
- The application MUST NOT silently suppress an API failure or proceed as though the request succeeded when it has not. [implied]
spec_artefacts.openapi:
openapi: 3.1.0
info:
  title: Quote and Joke Proxy API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: >
    Internal Next.js 14 proxy routes that front the Quote-of-the-Day and Joke
    third-party APIs. Consumed exclusively by the React client within the same
    application session.

paths:
  /api/quote:
    get:
      summary: Retrieve a single Quote of the Day from the upstream provider
      operationId: getQuote
      responses:
        '200':
          description: >
            A single quote was successfully retrieved from the upstream
            Quote-of-the-Day API and is returned to the client.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/QuoteResponse'
        '502':
          description: >
            The upstream Quote-of-the-Day API returned an error response or an
            unprocessable payload. The client should display a user-readable
            error message and offer a retry action.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorResponse'
        '504':
          description: >
            The upstream Quote-of-the-Day API did not respond within the
            allotted timeout window. The client should display a user-readable
            error message and offer a retry action.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorResponse'

  /api/joke:
    get:
      summary: Retrieve a single joke from the upstream provider
      operationId: getJoke
      responses:
        '200':
          description: >
            A single joke was successfully retrieved from the upstream Joke API
            and is returned to the client.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/JokeResponse'
        '502':
          description: >
            The upstream Joke API returned an error response or an
            unprocessable payload. The client should display a user-readable
            error message and offer a retry action.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorResponse'
        '504':
          description: >
            The upstream Joke API did not respond within the allotted timeout
            window. The client should display a user-readable error message and
            offer a retry action.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorResponse'

components:
  schemas:

    QuoteResponse:
      type: object
      description: >
        Normalised proxy representation of a single Quote-of-the-Day entry.
        The proxy layer maps the upstream provider's response shape to this
        contract; upstream field names are [implied] pending upstream API
        documentation.
      required:
        - quote
        - author
      properties:
        quote:
          type: string
          description: The full text of the quote to be displayed to the user.
          examples:
            - "The only way to do great work is to love what you do."
        author:
          type: string
          description: >
            The attributed author of the quote. [implied] May be a placeholder
            string such as "Unknown" when the upstream provider omits
            attribution.
          examples:
            - "Steve Jobs"
        date:
          type: string
          format: date
          description: >
            [implied] The calendar date for which this quote is designated,
            as supplied by the upstream provider. Omitted when the upstream
            provider does not return a date field.
          examples:
            - "2024-06-01"

    JokeResponse:
      type: object
      description: >
        Normalised proxy representation of a single joke entry. The proxy
        layer maps the upstream provider's response shape to this contract;
        upstream field names are [implied] pending upstream API documentation.
      required:
        - joke
      properties:
        joke:
          type: string
          description: >
            The full text of the joke to be displayed to the user. For
            two-part (setup/punchline) jokes the proxy concatenates both
            parts into a single displayable string. [implied]
          examples:
            - "Why don't scientists trust atoms? Because they make up everything."
        category:
          type: string
          description: >
            [implied] The thematic category of the joke as supplied by the
            upstream provider. Omitted when the upstream provider does not
            return a category field.
          examples:
            - "Science"

    ErrorResponse:
      type: object
      description: >
        Returned by the proxy on 502 and 504 responses. Carries a
        user-readable message suitable for direct display and a machine-readable
        code to support client-side retry logic.
      required:
        - message
        - code
      properties:
        message:
          type: string
          description: >
            A user-readable description of the failure, suitable for display
